title: Use codesize() as memory pointer for zero-length log4 — any valid pointer works
description: log4(offset, 0, ...) with length=0 reads zero bytes from memory regardless of offset. codesize() returns the contract's bytecode size — a valid but meaningless memory pointer when length is 0. Marginally cheaper than loading a constant 0x00 in some contexts, and signals intent: this pointer is never dereferenced.
pattern_after:
// codesize() == any valid uint, length 0 means offset is never read
log4(codesize(), 0x00, _TRANSFER_EVENT_SIGNATURE, from, to, id)
when_to_apply: Zero-length log4 calls where all event data is in topics
when_not_to_apply: Any log with non-zero length data
